intro: Boisduvalia tasmanica, also known as Tasmanian Boisduvalia, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Boisduvalia tasmanica include sesquiterpenoids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
